info.name: VitaReports
info.description: All VitaReports APIs. Suggested order: create-profile → update-manual-entry → extract-lab-reports / ingest-wearable-export → GET profile / lab-reports / wearable-observations (optional start/end) → health-snapshot GETs. Select Local env (baseUrl, patientId). For multipart requests, attach files from reassignmentcheckin/.

request.description: Profile plus manual entries. Run after create-profile / update-manual-entry.
List persisted lab/imaging reports for the patient (newest first). Run after extract-lab-reports.
Same as list, filtered to one report_type.
Attach wearable_export.xml. source_type=apple_health. Append + fingerprint dedupe (re-upload skips exact samples; conflicts kept). Response includes records_duplicate / records_future_skipped.

formdata.description: apple_health (add more values when new adapters exist)

query.description: e.g. heart_rate, spo2, steps, hrv_sdnn, sleep

request.description: List wearable observations (newest first, capped). Enable start/end for a UTC time window on end_at; optional metric_type. Run after ingest-wearable-export.

query.description: e.g. heart_rate, spo2, steps, hrv_sdnn, sleep

request.description: Wearable observations whose end_at falls between start and end (inclusive).
Composite health snapshot: vitals, adherence, symptoms, findings, care attention

exec: pm.test('Status is 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('patient_id equals expected', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.patient_id).to.eql(pm.environment.get('patientId') || pm.collectionVariables.get('patientId')); });
pm.test('recent_vitals is object', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.recent_vitals).to.be.an('object'); });
pm.test('medication_adherence is object', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.medication_adherence).to.be.an('object'); });
pm.test('symptoms is object', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.symptoms).to.be.an('object'); });
pm.test('hospital_findings is object', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.hospital_findings).to.be.an('object'); });
pm.test('care_attention is object', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.care_attention).to.be.an('object'); });

request.description: Point-in-time composite snapshot with custom as_of and adherence/symptom window

exec: pm.test('Status is 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('window_hours equals expected', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.window_hours).to.eql(48); });
pm.test('recent_vitals.vitals is array', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.recent_vitals.vitals).to.be.an('array'); });
pm.test('Status is 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('patient_id equals expected', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.patient_id).to.eql(pm.environment.get('patientId') || pm.collectionVariables.get('patientId')); });
pm.test('vitals is array', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.vitals).to.be.an('array'); });

pm.test('patient_id equals expected', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.patient_id).to.eql(pm.environment.get('patientId') || pm.collectionVariables.get('patientId')); });
pm.test('symptoms is array', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.symptoms).to.be.an('array'); });
pm.test('Status is 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('patient_id equals expected', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.patient_id).to.eql(pm.environment.get('patientId') || pm.collectionVariables.get('patientId')); });
pm.test('findings is array', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.findings).to.be.an('array'); });
pm.test('anomalies is array', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.anomalies).to.be.an('array'); });
pm.test('Status is 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('patient_id equals expected', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.patient_id).to.eql(pm.environment.get('patientId') || pm.collectionVariables.get('patientId')); });
pm.test('items is array', function () { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(json.items).to.be.an('array'); });
